systemAnalysis.systemImpact: This is a new, isolated workflow. No impact on existing system functionality is anticipated. It adds a new capability for engagement scoring.

executionSteps.description: Create the core workflow logic for the Engagement Scorer.

files.change: Create a new file to house the `EngagementScorer` class. This class will contain the logic to interact with an Ollama model to classify Mautic engagement history into 'hot', 'warm', or 'cold'.

executionSteps.description: Create TDD tests for the Engagement Scorer workflow.

files.change: Create a new test file with unit tests for the `EngagementScorer`. It will use 'unittest.mock.patch' to mock calls to the Ollama service, ensuring tests are independent and reliable.
Create a bash script that runs the unit tests for the new workflow to verify its correctness.

verificationScript: A script named `verify_script.sh` will be created and made executable. It activates the Python virtual environment and runs the newly created tests (`pytest tests/workflows/test_engagement_scorer.py`). A successful test run (exit code 0) verifies the change.
rollbackPlan: Remove the created Python files: `rm src/workflows/engagement_scorer.py tests/workflows/test_engagement_scorer.py`.
If any other files were modified, revert them using `git checkout -- <file_path>`.
